question: Does the model provide a final movement sequence using only the allowed directions (up, down, left, right) without diagonal moves or ambiguous actions?
Does the final text sequence successfully connect the Start point (S) to the End point (E) without crossing any walls when traced on the grid?
Is the final path length exactly 7 moves (matching the optimal solution)?
Does the final sequence start with exactly three consecutive 'right' moves (navigating the bottom corridor)?
Does the final sequence continue with exactly two consecutive 'up' moves (navigating the right-side vertical corridor)?
Does the final sequence end with 'left' followed by 'up' to reach the End point E?
In the generated images, are the maze structure (walls), Start position, and End position consistent with the original input image (no hallucinated openings or blockages)?
In the generated images, is the drawn path clearly visible, distinguishable from the background, and strictly contained within the open corridors (no wall overlaps)?
In the generated images, does the drawn path visually connect the Start zone (S) to the End zone (E) without any gaps or discontinuities?
Does the generated image correctly depict the initial path segment moving horizontally to the right along the bottom corridor?
Does the generated image correctly depict the vertical ascent through the open corridor on the right side of the maze?
Does the generated image correctly depict the final approach as a left turn followed by an upward move into the pink E zone?
If multiple intermediate images are generated, do the maze layout and path progress remain consistent across steps (no teleportation or changing walls)?
Does the model's reasoning correctly identify that moving directly 'up' from the Start is blocked, necessitating a move to the 'right'?
Does the model's reasoning identify the correct general strategy: traverse the bottom corridor to the right, go up the right side, and cut back left to E?
Does the reasoning explicitly avoid proposing moves through solid walls or claiming nonexistent openings (e.g., passing through the central barriers)?
Do the directional keywords in the text response (right, up, left) exactly match the direction of the path drawn in the generated images?
